questions.question: Explain the difference between online and offline password attacks, providing one example of each.
expectedPoints: Online attacks target live authentication systems (e.g., Hydra against FTP login)
Offline attacks work against captured password hashes (e.g., John the Ripper cracking /etc/shadow files)
Online attacks risk detection and account lockout
Offline attacks can be performed without network connectivity

questions.question: Describe the steps involved in a typical vulnerability exploitation process using Metasploit framework, from vulnerability identification to successful access.
expectedPoints: Vulnerability identification through previous enumeration phase
Searching for appropriate exploit module within Metasploit
Setting additional options and evasion techniques if needed
Executing the exploit and establishing session
